body:
# ASSUME

## Summary

Open agent-based electricity-market simulation toolbox. The card tracks it as a simulator reference for market behaviour and policy-analysis problems.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-401` as a candidate simulator for agent-based market simulation benchmarks.

## Caveats

- Code licence and data licence must be reviewed separately.
- This card must not be used as endorsement or evidence of operational performance without supporting sources.
title: ASSUME

body:
# bundesAPI Marktstammdaten API

## Summary

Open documentation and API-description work for public Marktstammdatenregister access.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-701` as an API-description and access-support reference for MaStR data.

## Caveats

- Licence status needs maintainer review.
- This card should not be treated as an official Bundesnetzagentur source unless that status is independently verified.
title: bundesAPI Marktstammdaten API

body:
# COIN-OR CBC

## Summary

Open-source mixed-integer programming solver from the COIN-OR ecosystem.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-901` as a candidate open MIP solver for
energy model cases where mixed-integer formulations need a reproducible solver
path.

## Caveats

- Energy-specific benchmark fit has not yet been reviewed in this repository.
- Packaging and licence details should be checked from the exact distribution
  used in any benchmark.
- This card does not treat CBC as a drop-in substitute for commercial solvers in
  all energy optimisation cases.
title: COIN-OR CBC

## Caveats

- This card must not be used as endorsement or evidence of operational performance without supporting sources.

body:
# HiGHS

## Summary

Open-source software for large-scale sparse linear programming, mixed-integer
programming, and quadratic programming.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-901` as a candidate open solver for energy
planning, dispatch, storage, and flexibility optimisation workloads.

## Caveats

- Energy-specific benchmark fit has not yet been reviewed in this repository.
- Solver performance claims should be workload-specific and reproducible.
- Code licence and data/model-instance licences must be reviewed separately.
title: HiGHS

## Summary

Julia modelling language and supporting package ecosystem for mathematical
optimization.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-901` as a solver-independent modelling layer

- Energy-specific benchmark fit has not yet been reviewed in this repository.
- Model reproducibility depends on solver choice, solver version, inputs, and
  execution environment.
- Code licence and model-instance/data licences must be reviewed separately.
title: JuMP

## Summary

Public Datasette-style access layer for exploring and querying Marktstammdatenregister data.

This contribution is linked to `EP-701` as an alternative public access interface for German energy asset registry data.

title: Marktstammdatenregister.dev

body:
# MaStR MCP Server

## Summary

MCP server for read access to the German Marktstammdatenregister. The project is tracked here because it makes public energy asset data more directly usable by AI assistants and agent workflows.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-701` as a candidate connector for AI-accessible energy asset registries.

## Caveats

- Code licence and data licence must be reviewed separately.
- API credentials and MaStR data reuse terms must be handled outside this repository.
- This card must not be used as endorsement or evidence of operational performance without supporting sources.
title: MaStR MCP Server

body:
# Official Marktstammdatenregister

## Summary

Official German electricity and gas market master-data register operated by the Bundesnetzagentur.

## Fit To Problem

This is the authoritative input source for MaStR access tools and registry connectors tracked under `EP-701`.

## Caveats

- Licence and reuse status must be reviewed from official source terms before derived data is republished.
- This card records the public source, not a claim that all MaStR data can be freely reused without restrictions.
title: Official Marktstammdatenregister

## Caveats

- Code licence and data licence must be reviewed separately.
- This card must not be used as endorsement or evidence of operational performance without supporting sources.

body:

## Summary

Python package for accessing, downloading, parsing, and processing German Marktstammdatenregister data.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-701` as a reference project for developer access to official German energy asset registry data.

## Caveats

- Code licence and data licence must be reviewed separately.
- The project wraps and processes MaStR data; it does not remove the need to respect the original MaStR data licence and attribution terms.
- This card must not be used as endorsement or evidence of operational performance without supporting sources.

body:
# Open Energy Modelling Tool Tracker

## Summary

Open Energy Transition's openmod-tracker collects open energy system modelling
tools from several upstream inventories and publishes repository, package,
documentation, and community-activity metrics through a dashboard.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-902` because it is a tool-level reference
for open energy modelling software health and adoption metrics.

It is complementary to this register. openmod-tracker is tool-first: it helps
answer which open modelling tools exist and how active or discoverable they
are. Open Energy Problems is problem-first: it asks which open references help
answer a particular grid, market, data, forecasting, or flexibility problem.

## Caveats

- Tool activity metrics do not prove that a tool solves a specific energy
  problem.
- The tracker focuses on energy system modelling tools, not all open energy
  datasets, registries, market disclosures, benchmarks, or AI-facing
  interfaces.
- Coverage of concrete geography-specific model instances, including German
  grid models, should be checked against the tracker inventory and upstream
  source lists.
title: Open Energy Modelling Tool Tracker

body:

Open short-term energy forecasting package and workflow. The card tracks it as a reproducible-method reference for forecasting benchmarks.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-101` as a reference project for short-term load and energy forecasting workflows.

## Caveats

- Code licence and data licence must be reviewed separately.
- This card must not be used as endorsement or evidence of operational performance without supporting sources.
title: OpenSTEF

body:
# Google OR-Tools

## Summary

Open-source optimization toolkit for combinatorial optimization, constraint
programming, routing, linear programming, and related problem classes.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-901` as candidate infrastructure for energy
scheduling, assignment, routing, and constraint-heavy benchmark cases.

## Caveats

- Energy-specific benchmark fit has not yet been reviewed in this repository.
- Some OR-Tools paths wrap or integrate with other solvers; each benchmark
  should record the backend actually used.
- This card does not imply suitability for all mathematical programming
  formulations used in energy systems.
title: Google OR-Tools

body:
# Pyomo

## Summary

Python-based open-source optimization modelling package for formulating,
solving, and analysing structured optimization models.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-901` as a solver-independent modelling layer
that can help energy models run against both open and commercial solvers.

## Caveats

- Energy-specific benchmark fit has not yet been reviewed in this repository.
- Model reproducibility depends on the solver, input data, parameters, and
  environment, not only on the modelling layer.
- Code licence and model-instance/data licences must be reviewed separately.
title: Pyomo

body:
# PyPSA

## Summary

Open framework for power-system analysis, optimisation, and simulation. The card tracks it as grid and system-modelling infrastructure relevant to open grid-stress problems.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-501` as a candidate simulator and modelling framework for reproducible grid-stress analysis.

## Caveats

- Code licence and data licence must be reviewed separately.
- This card must not be used as endorsement or evidence of operational performance without supporting sources.
title: PyPSA

body:
# SCIP

## Summary

Solver and framework for mixed-integer programming, mixed-integer nonlinear
programming, and constraint integer programming.

## Fit To Problem

This contribution is linked to `EP-901` as a candidate open solver stack for
harder energy optimisation cases where integer or nonlinear structure matters.

## Caveats

- SCIP version and dependency licences must be checked for each build.
- Energy-specific benchmark fit has not yet been reviewed in this repository.
- This card does not claim parity with any commercial solver for any given
  energy workload.
title: SCIP
